description: Shell command substitution $(...) strips trailing newlines from printf output. On lines 1614 and 1618, the pattern errors="${errors}$(printf 'CONFLICT: %s\n' {t})" loses the trailing newline because POSIX $() removes it. When multiple errors occur, messages concatenate without separators (e.g., 'CONFLICT: /path1CONFLICT: /path2'), causing the Python-side split('\n') on line 1624 to produce a single unsplittable line. The removeprefix('CONFLICT: ') then yields '/path1CONFLICT: /path2' as a single conflict path.
